pattern: Enumerate all 24 permutations of 4 digits and keep the lexicographically largest arrangement that forms a valid 24-hour time.
hints: With only 4 digits there are at most 4! = 24 permutations, which is small enough to check all of them exhaustively.
A valid time HH:MM requires HH in [0, 23] and MM in [0, 59]; check both conditions for each permutation.
Compare valid times as strings (they are zero-padded, so lexicographic order equals chronological order) and return the maximum.

edge_inputs.description: digits that form exactly one valid time
repeating digits that still allow a valid time
nearly max hours but only smaller minutes available
